target.title: [codex] fix webchat full-message reader for truncated history
target.body: ## Summary - Problem: Control UI could only open the already-loaded truncated assistant or tool text in the side reader, so long replies still felt like message loss. - Solution: add a bounded `chat.message.get` RPC that returns one display-normalized transcript entry by `sessionKey` + transcript `messageId`, then let the side reader upgrade truncated previews on demand. - What changed: gateway protocol/schema, transcript lookup helper, WebChat sidebar metadata flow, assistant/tool sidebar affordances, history-backed tool transcript id reuse, focused gateway/UI/docs coverage. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): the default `chat.history` payload cap, transcript storage shape, and inline chat bubble size policy. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [x] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #84651 - Related #53242 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Motivation - WebChat already had a sidebar affordance, but when history projection truncated the loaded text the sidebar repeated the same truncated preview. That made long assistant replies and tool outputs look unrecoverable after refresh. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI can now open the full display-normalized content for truncated assistant replies and tool outputs without raising the default `chat.history` payload cap. - Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the gateway server test harness and the Control UI sidebar flow exercised through focused gateway/UI tests after the patch. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Terminal output after the patch showed `Test Files 1 passed (1)` / `Tests 21 passed (21)` for `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts` and `Test Files 4 passed (4)` / `Tests 115 passed (115)` for the focused Control UI suite, including the new history-backed tool id assertion in `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts`. - Observed result after fix: Truncated history rows still stay lightweight in the main thread, but opening them in the side reader now upgrades to the full stored content when available, including tool outputs restored from history rows that only carry transcript identity in `__openclaw.id`, and shows an explicit unavailable state when the transcript row is oversized or no longer visible. - What was not tested: I did not run a manual browser screenshot/video capture against a live localhost Control UI session in this turn. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the patch, the existing source-level/UI behavior only reopened the already-truncated markdown or tool output preview, so the side reader could not recover omitted content after history reload. ### screenshot After ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: The sidebar open actions in `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts` and `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts` only reused the currently loaded `markdown` / `outputText`, while `chat.history` intentionally bounded those fields for UI stability. - Missing detection / guardrail: There was no stable per-message RPC for the UI to ask for one full transcript entry by identity. - Contributing context (if known): `chat.history` already carried transcript ids in `__openclaw`, but the UI was not using them for a targeted follow-up read, and tool sidebar cards initially only looked for top-level `messageId` instead of the same transcript metadata. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: truncated history preview upgrades to full content through `chat.message.get`, tool-details sidebar carries the same request metadata, history-backed tool messages reuse `__openclaw.id` as the stable fetch identity, and oversized transcript rows return an honest unavailable reason. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression spans protocol, transcript lookup, and UI affordance plumbing, so a seam-level gateway test plus focused UI interaction tests catches it without needing a full browser lane. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing `chat.history` truncation tests already prove the bounded history half of the behavior. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Control UI side reader can fetch full assistant/tool content on demand when the visible history preview was truncated. - If the stored transcript row is oversized or otherwise unavailable, the side reader now says so explicitly instead of silently repeating the truncated preview. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: [history preview truncated] -> [open sidebar] -> [same truncated preview] After: [history preview truncated] -> [open sidebar] -> [chat.message.get by messageId] -> [full content or explicit unavailable state] ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `chat.message.get` is read-only, uses the existing `operator.read` scope, stays inside the session/transcript ownership boundary, and returns structured unavailable reasons instead of widening history payloads. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local source checkout - Model/provider: not model-specific; transcript/gateway/UI behavior - Integration/channel (if any): Control UI / WebChat - Relevant config (redacted): default bounded `chat.history` behavior with focused test harness config ### Steps 1. Seed a session transcript with a long assistant message whose `chat.history` projection is truncated. 2. Load history, capture the truncated preview, then request `chat.message.get` for the same transcript `messageId`. 3. Open the assistant/tool sidebar affordance and verify the sidebar payload carries the full-message request metadata. ### Expected - `chat.history` stays bounded. - `chat.message.get` returns the full display-normalized entry when available. - Oversized rows report an explicit unavailable reason. ### Actual - Matches expected after the patch. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What I personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Ran focused gateway/UI Vitest coverage for the new RPC, sidebar metadata flow, and history-backed tool transcript-id reuse. - Ran `pnpm build`, targeted `tsgo`, and targeted `oxlint` after the patch.

target.title: fix(podman): mount host ~/.claude into setup container
target.body:
## Summary
- Setup wizard fails with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` under rootless Podman because `~/.claude` is never mounted into the onboard container, so `readClaudeCliCredentials` cannot find `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json`.
- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` is intentionally stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`), so the env workaround does not survive managed runs.
- Bind-mount the host's `~/.claude` read-only into the **setup container only** when `.credentials.json` exists. Gateway and other non-setup launch paths are untouched.
- Reuse the existing `ensure_private_existing_dir_owned_by_user` + `ensure_private_existing_regular_file_owned_by_user` guards on both `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR` and `$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json` before mounting — rejects symlinks, wrong owner, and group/other-writable sources.
- **Soft-fail**: when guards reject the source, emit a warning and skip the mount; setup still proceeds so users configuring a non-Claude provider, or those with no `~/.claude` at all, are never blocked by Claude credential hygiene.
- Use the shared SELinux relabel (`,z`) instead of the exclusive `,Z` so the host's own Claude CLI keeps access to its credentials after the setup container exits.
- Allow overriding the host source via `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR`.

Fixes #81899

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Rootless Podman setup wizard run from a host that has a working `~/.claude/.credentials.json` fails at "Anthropic Claude CLI" onboarding with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` (#81899), because `scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh` never mounted the host `.claude` directory into the setup container. After this patch the setup container gets a conditional, read-only, shared-SELinux-relabel bind mount of the host `~/.claude`, soft-gated on private-owner + symlink + permission checks, scoped to `RUN_SETUP == true` so non-setup gateway launches are unaffected, with the validation failing to a warning rather than aborting setup.

- **Real environment tested:** Fresh Fedora 43 Cloud Edition VM (DigitalOcean droplet, redacted IP), SELinux **Enforcing**, kernel `6.17.1-300.fc43.x86_64`, `podman 5.6.2`, rootless non-root `tester` user (uid 1000) with linger enabled, real `~/.claude/.credentials.json` (mode 600, owner tester), patched openclaw checkout at HEAD `5541bad4`. No mocks of the production validation helpers; only `podman` was wrapped for argv capture in the non-real-podman steps. The real-mount step uses upstream `registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal` to confirm the SELinux `,z` relabel.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `git fetch && git checkout 5541bad4` on the Fedora droplet as `tester`
  2. `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
  3. Positive setup with `~/.claude` perms 700/600 owned by tester — assert mount argv emitted, no warning
  4. Setup compatibility (P1 fix): chmod 640 file / chmod 770 dir / symlink file / symlink dir — assert warning printed AND setup `podman run` still invoked (exit 0, mount absent)
  5. Non-setup `launch` with loose perms — assert exit 0, gateway container starts
  6. Real container: `podman run --rm -v $HOME/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal /bin/bash -c "[ -r /home/node/.claude/.credentials.json ]"`
  7. Post-exit host check: `claude auth status` on the host

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live terminal output from the live Fedora 43 rootless Podman + SELinux **Enforcing** run on commit `5541bad4`. Token, email, and org UUID redacted in-place; hostname and IP redacted.

```
=== ENV (soft-fail commit 5541bad4) ===

Fedora Linux 43 (Cloud Edition) / SELinux: Enforcing / podman 5.6.2
openclaw SHA: 5541bad46f0d3354ad502d21701486005ed2519a

=== HAPPY PATH — perms 700/600 owner=tester — mount EMITTED, no warning ===

700 tester:tester /home/tester/.claude
600 tester:tester /home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json

==podman-invoked==
argc=30
  run
  --pull=never
  --rm
  -it
  --init
  --userns=keep-id
  --user
  1000:1000
  -e
  HOME=/home/node
  -e
  TERM=xterm-256color
  -e
  BROWSER=echo
  -e
  NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/home/node/.openclaw/.npm
  -e
  OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1
  --env-file
  /home/tester/.openclaw/.token.env.c3IZUX
  -v
  /home/tester/.openclaw:/home/node/.openclaw:rw,Z
  -v
  /home/tester/.openclaw/workspace:/home/node/.openclaw/workspace:rw,Z
  -v
  /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z
  openclaw:local
  node
  dist/index.js
  onboard
```

```
=== STEP 3 (P1 fix) — setup + chmod 640 creds — WARN, skip mount, setup CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials file: expected owner-only permissions (/home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
exit=1 podman invocations (must be 1)

=== STEP 4 (P1 fix) — setup + chmod 770 dir — WARN, skip, CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials directory: group/other writable (/home/tester/.claude)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
podman invocations: 1

=== STEP 5 (P1 fix) — setup + symlinked .credentials.json — WARN, skip, CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials file: symlinks are not allowed (/home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
podman invocations: 1

=== STEP 6 (P1 fix) — setup + symlinked dir — WARN, skip, CONTINUES ===

warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.
         Unsafe claude credentials directory: symlinks are not allowed (/home/tester/.claude)
         Non-Claude providers will still onboard; for Anthropic Claude CLI, fix permissions/ownership of /home/tester/.claude and re-run setup.
==podman-invoked==
podman invocations: 1

=== STEP 7 — NON-setup (gateway) + loose creds (644) — no abort, no mount, exit 0 ===

Container openclaw started: http://127.0.0.1:18789/
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw dashboard --no-open
podman exec -it openclaw openclaw devices approve --latest  # if pairing required
podman logs -f openclaw
For auto-start/restarts, use: ./scripts/podman/setup.sh --quadlet (Quadlet + systemd user service).

=== STEP 8 — setup happy path POST-recovery — mount re-emitted ===


=== STEP 9 — real podman ,z mount — file readable in container ===

time="2026-05-18T20:56:01Z" level=warning msg="Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root)"
time="2026-05-18T20:56:01Z" level=warning msg="Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs"
READABLE=yes

=== STEP 10 — host claude auth status post-container ===
```

- **Observed result after fix:**
  - **Setup happy path** (dir 700, file 600, owner=tester): `-v /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` emitted in the podman argv exactly once, no warning.
  - **Setup P1 compatibility fix** (unsafe credentials no longer abort setup): each of `file 640`, `dir 770`, `file is symlink`, `dir is symlink` prints `warning: skipping host Claude credentials mount; setup will continue without it.` plus the exact private-check reason (`"expected owner-only permissions"`, `"group/other writable"`, `"symlinks are not allowed"`), and setup still invokes `podman run` (mock-podman shows exactly 1 invocation per case with the `.claude` mount absent). The prior review's `[P1] Preserve setup when Claude creds are unsafe — scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh:537-539` is closed: unsafe credentials no longer kill setup; non-Claude provider selection in the onboarding TUI remains reachable.
  - **Non-setup compatibility**: with loose file perms (644), symlinked `.claude`, and missing `.credentials.json` the gateway launch path completes with exit code 0; no validation abort and no `.claude` mount appears in the gateway podman argv — the prior review's `[P1] Keep Claude credential validation inside setup` stays closed.
  - **Real `podman run` with the PR's `,z` mount**: container reads `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json` (`READABLE=yes`).
  - **Host `claude auth status` after container exit** reports `loggedIn: true`, confirming the host's own Claude CLI continues to authenticate (load-bearing for `,z` shared relabel vs `,Z` exclusive).

- **What was not tested:** End-to-end onboarding TUI through the production `openclaw` container image was not exercised because the proof above already covers the load-bearing wiring (script-level mount construction, soft-fail behavior, real SELinux relabel against a real `podman run`, post-exit host auth). A maintainer with a Fedora rootless Podman host can run `./scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch setup`, pick a provider inside the wizard, and confirm the end-to-end happy path; the diff is a small shell-script patch with no schema or migration risk.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
- [x] Live Fedora 43 rootless Podman + SELinux enforcing setup happy path — mount argv emitted
- [x] Live Fedora 43 setup with unsafe creds (P1 fix) — warning emitted, setup continues, mount absent
- [x] Live Fedora 43 non-setup gateway path with loose perms / symlink / missing creds — no abort, no mount
- [x] Real `podman run -v $HOME/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` — container reads credentials file
- [x] Host `claude auth status` after container exit — still logged in (proves `,z` shared relabel, not `,Z` exclusive)

## AI-assisted disclosure
- [x] AI-assisted (Claude Code, Opus 4.7).
- [x] I understand what the code does.
- [x] Human-run live proof on a real Fedora SELinux-enforcing rootless Podman host is included above.

target.title: fix(ui): add agent selector to dreaming tab
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: the Control UI Dreaming tab had no agent selector, so users could not switch Dreaming context between agent workspaces.
- Why it matters: Dreaming artifacts (`DREAMS.md`, short-term recall, schedules, memory state) are agent-specific, so the tab was effectively stuck to the current session context.
- What changed: added an agent selector to the Dreaming tab chrome and wired it to the same preferred-session-per-agent logic used by chat session controls.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no backend dreaming behavior, routing behavior, or storage behavior was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63558
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Dreaming tab lacked an agent selector, preventing users from switching agent contexts there.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts > dreaming view > renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       16 passed (16)
```
- Observed result after fix: the Dreaming tab now renders an agent selector when multiple agents are available, preselects the active agent, and triggers agent-context switching through the same preferred-session resolution used elsewhere.
- What was not tested: end-to-end browser interaction against a live multi-agent gateway.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Dreaming used its own tab chrome and never reused the shared agent-selection/session-switching logic used in chat.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Dreaming view tests did not assert presence of an agent selector for multi-agent setups.
- Contributing context (if known): the rest of Control UI already assumed agent-specific contexts for many views, so Dreaming lagged behind that UX pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when multiple agents are available, Dreaming shows an agent selector, reflects the active agent, and calls back when the selection changes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the missing feature is entirely in the Dreaming tab view/chrome layer.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds `renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts`.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Control UI Dreaming tab now shows an agent selector when multiple agent contexts are available, so users can switch Dreaming context directly from the tab.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI / Dreaming tab

### Steps

1. Render Dreaming with multiple available agent options.
2. Verify an agent selector is present.
3. Verify the current agent is selected.
4. Change the selection and verify the callback fires with the target agent id.

### Expected

- Dreaming shows the current agent and allows agent switching.

### Actual

- Dreaming view test passes with the new selector behavior.
- `tsgo:core` passes.
- `lint:core` passes.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the selector appears in the Dreaming tab chrome.
- Verified the selected agent is reflected in the UI.
- Verified agent changes route through preferred-session-per-agent logic.
- Verified view test, `tsgo:core`, and `lint:core` all pass.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the selector could route users to an unexpected session for an agent.
  - Mitigation: it uses the same preferred-session-per-agent resolution logic already used in chat controls.
- Risk: single-agent setups could get cluttered.
  - Mitigation: the selector renders only when more than one agent option is available.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  gateway-methods  src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts (27 tests) 89ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  27 passed (27)
    Start at  00:25:12
    Duration  470ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Selecting an agent in the Dreaming tab called `switchChatSession()` but `loadDreamingStatus` and `loadDreamDiary` still sent `{}` to `doctor.memory.*`, showing the default agent's data regardless of selection. The tab could display DREAMS.md and memory state for the wrong agent.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 27/27 gateway method tests pass. TypeScript `tsc --noEmit` reports no errors across core and extensions configs.
**Observed result after fix:** `onSelectAgent` sets `selectedAgentId` on state, immediately reloads `dreamingStatus` and `dreamDiary` scoped to the selected agent. Gateway handlers accept optional `agentId` param with backward-compatible fallback to `resolveDefaultAgentId` when omitted.
**What was not tested:** Live multi-agent Control UI browser session; gateway handler routing is verified at the unit level through the existing 27 gateway method tests.

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #82321
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A daily reset with `atHour` is a wall-clock policy. Users should not need to send a new message after the reset boundary before stale session context is cleared. The maintenance sweep should also honor the current runtime config after hot reloads, not the startup snapshot.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Daily reset freshness was only evaluated through session-use paths. If no message arrived after `atHour`, the stale session could remain active even though the configured reset time had passed. The initial scheduler also captured the startup config, which meant hot reloads could leave the sweep using stale reset policy until restart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - A stale daily session resets during a scheduler sweep without a new message.
  - A fresh daily session is left alone.
  - Implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are not cut by the default daily policy.
  - Sessions with active runs are skipped.
  - Scheduler sweeps use current runtime config after a reset-policy change.
- Existing coverage before this patch covered stale detection when called, but not proactive scheduled execution or reload-aware maintenance behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Configured daily session resets now happen on schedule during gateway uptime, even if no message arrives after the reset boundary. The public docs now state that proactive rollover can happen from gateway maintenance and that implicit provider-owned CLI sessions keep continuity unless reset is explicit.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
daily reset boundary
  -> gateway maintenance sweep
  -> read current runtime config
  -> skip active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions
  -> stale daily sessions reset through existing reset service
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `session.reset.mode=daily`, `session.reset.atHour=4`

### Steps

1. Create a session store entry with `sessionStartedAt` before the daily reset boundary.
2. Run the daily reset scheduler scan with `nowMs` after the reset boundary.
3. Confirm the reset callback runs for the stale session.
4. Repeat with a fresh post-boundary session and confirm it is not reset.
5. Mark a stale session key active and confirm it is skipped.
6. Add provider-owned CLI continuity under the implicit reset policy and confirm it is skipped.
7. Change the scheduler's runtime config from a noon reset boundary to a 4 AM reset boundary and confirm the next sweep uses the new policy.

### Expected

Stale daily sessions reset without inbound traffic; fresh, active, and implicitly provider-owned CLI sessions remain unchanged; config reloads affect future scheduler sweeps.

### Actual

The focused scheduler and gateway maintenance tests match that behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Focused scheduler and maintenance test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.
- Docs updated in:
  - `docs/concepts/session.md`
  - `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`
  - `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale daily reset scheduler path, fresh-session no-op path, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, runtime-config reload path, gateway maintenance timer lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: scheduler skips overlapping sweeps; shutdown/runtime cleanup clears the timer; type gates pass.
- Not verified: a live gateway instance naturally crossing the configured reset hour.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the gateway now scans configured session stores every minute.
- Mitigation: scans are bounded to discovered session stores, skip overlapping runs, and only call reset for stale daily sessions.
- Risk: deployments that assumed lazy daily resets may observe earlier reset behavior.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock expectation for `mode: daily` and `atHour`.
- Risk: hot config changes could otherwise make the scheduler use stale reset policy.
- Mitigation: future sweeps call the gateway runtime config getter before scanning.

target.title: Skip empty sherpa structured transcripts
target.body:
## Summary

- Treat structured `sherpa-onnx-offline` JSON with an empty `text` field as an empty transcript instead of falling back to raw stdout.
- Preserve normal sherpa JSON extraction for non-empty transcript output, including final-line JSON after startup logs.
- Add direct CLI-runner and auto-detected audio regression coverage.

Closes #84660.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Moonshine/sherpa can emit a structured JSON object where `text` is empty. Before this patch, OpenClaw treated that as unrecognized sherpa output, fell back to the raw JSON string, and could pass that JSON to the LLM as a voice transcript.

Real environment tested:
Local macOS worktree with bundled Node.js `v24.14.0`, repository dependencies, and a fake executable named `sherpa-onnx-offline` invoked through the real CLI media runner.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs /private/tmp/openclaw-84660-proof/proof.ts
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts
git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
```

Evidence after fix:
The standalone runtime proof invokes the real `runCliEntry()` path with a fake `sherpa-onnx-offline` binary. Empty structured JSON no longer returns a transcript, while non-empty structured JSON still extracts normally:
```json
{
  "emptySherpaJsonReturnedTranscript": false,
  "emptySherpaJsonText": null,
  "nonEmptySherpaJsonText": "hello from sherpa",
  "nonEmptySherpaJsonKind": "audio.transcription"
}
```

Targeted tests:
```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       52 passed (52)
```

Observed result after fix:
Empty sherpa/moonshine JSON is skipped before it can become `ctx.Transcript` or an `audio.transcription` output, so the serialized Discord voice lane will not enqueue an agent turn for that empty segment. Non-empty sherpa JSON remains accepted.

What was not tested:
I did not run a live Discord voice session or the full repository test suite. The proof uses the real media CLI runner with a fake sherpa executable and targeted media-understanding tests for the affected normalization and auto-detect paths.

## Attribution

If this PR is squashed or reworked, please preserve the commit author attribution for Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>, or include:

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Refs #77969
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TTS can turn emoji and decorative symbols into noisy spoken names. Users who prefer cleaner voice output need a config-level way to strip those characters before synthesis without changing default behavior for everyone else.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `tts-skip-v3`, commit `06545b841d08`, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (`extensions/microsoft` -> `node-edge-tts` -> `speech.platform.bing.com`). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- After-fix evidence:
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
  # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
  TS
  {
    "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
    "commit": "06545b841d08",
    "provider": "microsoft",
    "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
    "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
    "observed": [
      {
        "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
        "bytes": 39456,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      },
      {
        "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
        "bytes": 36288,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      }
    ],
    "emptyAfterStrip": {
      "success": false,
      "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
      "provider": null
    },
    "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
  }

  $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
  ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       32 passed (32)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       14 passed (14)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing `✓`, `©`, and `🫠`; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The original implementation filtered only some TTS entry points and did not guard the post-filter text before synthesis. The telephony path computed filtered text for setup but still passed the original text into provider synthesis, and emoji-only input could become an empty provider request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
  - stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
  - telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can enable `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` to strip emoji/symbol characters before speech synthesis. Unset or `false` preserves existing behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
text -> effective TTS config -> optional emoji/symbol strip -> empty guard -> provider synthesis
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: live proof speech-provider plugin plus focused Vitest coverage
- Integration/channel: speech-core file, stream, telephony, and TTS tool paths
- Relevant config: `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols=true|false`

### Steps

1. Run the live proof script shown above.
2. Run focused speech-core tests.
3. Run focused TTS tool tests.
4. Run test and production type gates.

### Expected

Enabled stripping removes emoji/symbols before provider synthesis and blocks empty-after-strip requests. Disabled mode preserves original text.

### Actual

The proof and tests match the expected behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Live speech-provider output for enabled, disabled, stream, telephony, and empty-after-strip paths.
- Focused speech-core test output.
- Focused TTS tool test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: enabled/disabled filtering, file synthesis, stream synthesis, telephony synthesis, empty-after-strip guard, adjacent TTS tool behavior.
- Edge cases checked: complete emoji/symbol sequences, emoji-only input, telephony provider request text, stream provider no-call behavior.
- Not verified: live vendor-specific pronunciation across every external provider.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: optional new config `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`; no change required when unset.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unicode stripping removes characters a user wanted spoken.
- Mitigation: behavior is opt-in and disabled by default.
- Risk: filtering differs across TTS paths.
- Mitigation: shared preparation is used before file, stream, and telephony synthesis, with regression tests for each path.

target.title: fix #84745: scope Google preview model normalization to Google providers only
target.body:
## Summary

`normalizeAgentModelRefForConfig` in `src/config/model-input.ts` was calling `normalizeGooglePreviewModelId` on the model suffix regardless of provider, causing non-Google providers (e.g. `litellm/gemini-3-flash`) to be incorrectly rewritten to preview IDs (`litellm/gemini-3-flash-preview`). This broke cron preflight allowlist validation for custom LiteLLM-backed providers.

## Changes

- Added provider check in `normalizeAgentModelRefForConfig()`: Google preview model normalization is now only applied when the provider is `google`, `google-gemini-cli`, or `google-vertex`
- Matches the existing pattern in `normalizeBuiltInProviderModelId()` in `src/agents/model-ref-shared.ts`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Non-Google provider model refs (e.g. litellm/gemini-3-flash) are no longer incorrectly normalized to preview IDs (litellm/gemini-3-flash-preview)
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node.js v22.22.0, worktree SHA f4b4122d
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node proof_repro_84745.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix**:
```
PASS: litellm/gemini-3-flash stays unchanged (non-Google provider)
  Actual:   litellm/gemini-3-flash
  Expected: litellm/gemini-3-flash

  (Buggy version produced: litellm/gemini-3-flash-preview)

PASS: litellm/gemini-3.1-pro stays unchanged (non-Google provider)
  Actual:   litellm/gemini-3.1-pro
  Expected: litellm/gemini-3.1-pro

PASS: google/gemini-3-flash gets normalized to preview
  Actual:   google/gemini-3-flash-preview
  Expected: google/gemini-3-flash-preview

PASS: google-vertex/gemini-3-flash gets normalized to preview
  Actual:   google-vertex/gemini-3-flash-preview
  Expected: google-vertex/gemini-3-flash-preview

PASS: google-gemini-cli/gemini-3.1-pro gets normalized to preview
  Actual:   google-gemini-cli/gemini-3.1-pro-preview
  Expected: google-gemini-cli/gemini-3.1-pro-preview

PASS: openai/gpt-4o stays unchanged
  Actual:   openai/gpt-4o
  Expected: openai/gpt-4o

PASS: gemini-3-flash without provider stays unchanged
  Actual:   gemini-3-flash
  Expected: gemini-3-flash

PASS: normalizeAgentModelMapForConfig preserves litellm keys, normalizes google keys
  Fixed map keys: litellm/gemini-3-flash, litellm/gemini-3.1-pro, google/gemini-3-flash-preview

=== Results: 8 passed, 0 failed ===
```
- **Observed result after fix**: All 8 test cases pass. Non-Google provider model refs are preserved unchanged. Google provider model refs are still correctly normalized to preview IDs.
- **What was not tested**: Live cron preflight with actual LiteLLM provider configuration (requires running OpenClaw gateway with custom provider config)

## Test plan

- [ ] proof_repro_84745.mjs passes locally (8/8 tests)
- [ ] Verify `google/gemini-3-flash` still normalizes to `google/gemini-3-flash-preview`
- [ ] Verify `litellm/gemini-3-flash` stays as `litellm/gemini-3-flash`
- [ ] Verify existing model selection tests still pass

target.title: fix(agents): inherit subagent thinking defaults
target.body:
## Summary

- Subagent spawns now inherit the requester session's persisted thinking level when the spawn request and agent config do not specify one.
- The fallback order is explicit spawn thinking, requester agent subagent default, target agent subagent default, global subagent default, then requester session thinking.
- Existing explicit/configured thinking behavior is unchanged.

- Fixes #55790
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A user-selected session thinking level should carry into subagents by default. Before this change, a parent session could run with one thinking setting while a child session silently lost that setting unless the spawn request or config repeated it.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_spawn(runtime="subagent")` now preserves requester session thinking as the final fallback when no explicit or configured subagent thinking is set.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-subagent-thinking-defaults`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn-applies-thinking-default.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured from the patched branch:

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed
  Tests       5 passed
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The spawn-path coverage persists the inherited requester `thinkingLevel` onto the child session, while explicit/configured thinking continues to take precedence.
- What was not tested: A live provider-backed subagent run.


## Root Cause (if applicable)

The subagent thinking resolver only knew about explicit spawn input and configured subagent defaults. It did not receive requester session thinking, so it had no way to preserve the caller's persisted thinking level.

target.title: perf(cli): lazy-load agents actions for help
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw agents --help` imported the full agents action barrel and paid heavy startup cost on a help-only path.
- Solution: keep the Commander skeleton static, but lazy-load agent and agents action modules only from their action callbacks.
- What changed: direct lazy imports for agents add/list/bind/unbind/delete/set-identity and agent run; `agents-list` routed command now imports the list module directly; cold-import tests cover `agents --help`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no agents command behavior, docs link, option, routing, or generic subcommand help changes.

## Motivation

- Before this patch, `agents --help` measured at ~1.38s average and ~522MB RSS on the built CLI in this worktree, while root/browser help stayed ~23ms/~46MB.
- The import graph made command discovery/help paths pay for agents action/runtime modules even when no action runs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Auth / tokens

- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw agents --help` cold-start import/runtime cost.
- Real environment tested: local macOS worktree, Node v24.14.0, built `openclaw.mjs` entry.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm build`; `node dist/entry.js agents --help`; `pnpm test:startup:bench -- --case agentsHelp --runs 3 --warmup 1 --json --output .artifacts/perf-agents-help-after.json`.
- Evidence after fix: benchmark after patch averaged 361.2ms, p95 433.4ms, RSS avg 321.7MB.
- Observed result after fix: help output unchanged and startup/RSS materially reduced from before baseline.
- What was not tested: full `pnpm test` suite.
- Before evidence: before benchmark averaged 1382.0ms, p95 1423.1ms, RSS avg 522.4MB.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `register.agent.ts` statically imported `agent-via-gateway` and the `commands/agents.ts` barrel; the barrel re-exported add/delete/bind/list/config modules, so help registration loaded action/runtime modules.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no cold-import test covered `agents --help`.
- Contributing context: root/browser help have precomputed fast paths; agents help relies on the general registrar path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cli/help-cold-imports.test.ts`, `src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: `agents --help` does not import agent/agents action modules, while action paths still call the same command functions.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it tests the registrar import boundary directly without process-level timing noise.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for agents help cold imports.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw agents --help` starts faster and uses less memory. Help text and command behavior are unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
agents --help -> register.agent.ts -> commands/agents.ts barrel -> action modules loaded

After:
agents --help -> register.agent.ts -> help skeleton only
action run  -> callback -> direct import of exactly one action module
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node v24.14.0, local pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A

- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. `pnpm build`
2. `node dist/entry.js agents --help > .artifacts/agents-help-after.txt`
3. `diff -u .artifacts/agents-help-before.txt .artifacts/agents-help-after.txt`
4. `pnpm test:startup:bench -- --case agentsHelp --runs 3 --warmup 1 --json --output .artifacts/perf-agents-help-after.json`

### Expected

- Help output unchanged.
- `agents --help` avoids loading heavy agents action modules.
- Startup time/RSS materially drop.

### Actual

- Help output diff was empty.
- `agents --help` benchmark improved from 1382.0ms avg / 522.4MB RSS avg to 361.2ms avg / 321.7MB RSS avg.
- Cold-import tests pass.

## Evidence

## Human Verification (required)

  - `pnpm build`

- Edge cases checked: parent `agents` default action still calls list; lazy-loaded action failures still route through runtime error handling; routed `agents-list` avoids the barrel.

target.title: Policy: add tool metadata conformance
target.body:
# Policy: add tool metadata conformance

## Summary

This PR extends the bundled Policy plugin from channel conformance into governed tool metadata. Policy remains a read-only conformance layer over existing OpenClaw surfaces: `policy.jsonc` states requirements, `TOOLS.md` is observed as evidence, and `policy check` / `doctor --lint` report drift without mutating the workspace.

- Adds `tools.requireMetadata` policy support for `risk`, `sensitivity`, and `owner`.
- Observes `TOOLS.md` declarations as tool evidence, including source `oc://TOOLS.md/tools/<id>` references.
- Adds doctor/policy findings for missing or unknown tool metadata.
- Keeps `policy check`, `policy check --json`, `policy watch`, and `doctor --lint` read-only.
- Adds `policy watch` for repeated accepted-attestation drift checks, with `--once` for CI/supervisor probes.
- Leaves runtime tool enforcement out of scope for a dedicated follow-up PR.

## Quick Start

Enable the bundled Policy plugin and author a policy requirement:

```bash
openclaw plugins enable policy
```

```jsonc
{
  "tools": {
    "requireMetadata": ["risk", "sensitivity", "owner"],
  },
}
```

A matching `TOOLS.md` declaration should carry the required metadata:

```md
## Tools
- deploy: risk: critical sensitivity: restricted owner: ops
```

Run policy-only checks during authoring:

```bash
openclaw policy check
openclaw policy check --json
```

Watch accepted attestation drift for long-running supervisors or CI probes:

```bash
openclaw policy watch --once
openclaw policy watch --json --once
```

The same findings are available through `openclaw doctor --lint` when the Policy plugin is enabled.

## Configuration

Policy config remains under `plugins.entries.policy.config`:

```jsonc
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "policy": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "enabled": true,
          "path": "policy.jsonc",
          "workspaceRepairs": false,
          "expectedHash": "sha256:...",
          "expectedAttestationHash": "sha256:...",
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Audit State

`policy check --json` emits the stable audit tuple:

```text
policy hash + evidence hash + findings hash + clean result -> attestation hash
```

The tool evidence is part of the workspace evidence hash. A clean result can be accepted by recording `attestation.policy.hash` as `expectedHash` and `attestation.attestationHash` as `expectedAttestationHash`.

## Findings

This PR adds these policy findings:

| Finding | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `policy/tools-missing-owner` | A governed tool declaration is missing owner metadata. |
| `policy/tools-missing-risk-level` | A governed tool declaration is missing risk metadata. |
| `policy/tools-unknown-risk-level` | A governed tool declaration uses an unknown risk value. |
| `policy/tools-missing-sensitivity-token` | A governed tool declaration is missing sensitivity metadata. |
| `policy/tools-unknown-sensitivity-token` | A governed tool declaration uses an unknown sensitivity value. |

Findings identify both sides of the decision: `target` points to the observed `TOOLS.md` declaration and `requirement` points to the authored policy requirement.

## Out Of Scope

This PR does not add runtime tool enforcement, approval UI, supervisor protocol changes, gateway protocol changes, Swift protocol model changes, or a cross-plugin `oc-path` runtime dependency. `policy watch` reports attestation drift from the existing policy check contract; it does not enforce tool calls at runtime. Runtime enforcement should land separately with fail-closed schema validation for invalid policy artifacts.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: The bundled Policy plugin can now enforce authored `TOOLS.md` metadata conformance for tool `risk`, `sensitivity`, and `owner` fields through read-only policy and doctor diagnostics, and `policy watch` reports accepted-attestation drift from the same evidence tuple.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout on PR head `b24746d0dd9f5f8785d69d4c68d361adc0108ad1`, compared against `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/policy-state.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts test/release-check.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --project tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/stage-bundled-plugin-runtime.test.ts -t "stages bundled dist plugins as runtime wrappers without linking plugin node_modules"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/loader.test.ts -t "refreshes bundled plugin-sdk aliases without deleting the shared alias directory"`

Evidence after fix: Focused Policy plugin tests passed for policy state hashing, doctor registration, CLI reporting, JSON output, `policy watch` stale-attestation behavior, bullet and heading TOOLS.md declarations, invalid metadata keys, and read-only doctor/policy reporting. The packaging-focused smoke tests passed after removing the unnecessary `oc-path` package alias changes.

Observed result after fix: The focused Policy/release run passed 4 files and 87 tests. The two package-alias smoke tests each passed with unrelated tests skipped. `git diff --check` and `tsgo` passed.

What was not tested: Runtime policy enforcement, approval metadata propagation, approval UI, supervisor protocol, gateway protocol, and Swift protocol surfaces are intentionally not part of this PR.

## Related

- Channels-first policy conformance: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80407
- Model, network, and MCP conformance examples: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80783
- Runtime audit metadata and attestation enforcement: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/81104

<!-- policy-stack-links:start -->

## Policy stack links

This PR is part of the Policy 1.0 proof stack:

1. #80407 — Policy: add conformance system with channel checks
2. #80056 — Policy: add tool metadata conformance
3. #80783 — Policy: add model, network, and MCP conformance checks
4. #81104 — Policy: add runtime audit metadata and attestation enforcement
5. #81974 — Policy: add secrets auth provenance checks
6. #81981 — Policy: add gateway exposure checks

<!-- policy-stack-links:end -->

target.title: fix(simple-completion): sanitize Google thinking payload for unknown Gemini aliases (fixes #84688)
target.body:
## Background

Reported in #84688: running `openclaw model run` with `--transport=local` against a Google Gemini model whose ID is not recognized as Gemini 3 Flash by pi-ai (notably `gemini-flash-latest`) causes Google's generative-ai endpoint to reject the request because the outbound payload contains `thinkingConfig.thinkingBudget: -1`.

The normal agent runtime is unaffected because the embedded runner wraps the stream with `createGoogleThinkingPayloadWrapper` (see `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts`). The lean local simple-completion path goes through `completeWithPreparedSimpleCompletionModel` → `prepareModelForSimpleCompletion` → pi-ai `completeSimple`, which bypasses that wrapper.

## Root cause

`@earendil-works/pi-ai@0.75.3`'s `getGoogleBudget` only matches explicit Gemini 3 Flash IDs (`/gemini-3(?:\.\d+)?-flash/`); for unknown Gemini reasoning aliases it returns `-1`. `prepareModelForSimpleCompletion` previously only registered custom stream wrappers when pi-ai had no api provider, when an explicit OpenClaw transport (`request.proxy`, `request.tls`, `localService`) was configured, or for Anthropic Vertex. Plain `google-generative-ai` models therefore reached pi-ai's built-in Google stream unchanged.

## Fix

Register an OpenClaw-owned simple-completion API alias (`openclaw-google-generative-ai-simple`) for `google-generative-ai` models. The alias wraps pi-ai's native `streamSimple` with the shared `sanitizeGoogleThinkingPayload` helper (which already understands `gemini-flash-latest` via `isGoogleGemini3FlashModel` and maps thinking levels / strips invalid negative budgets). pi-ai is not modified.

## Tests

- New `src/agents/google-simple-completion-stream.test.ts` covers `gemini-flash-latest` × `thinking: off | low | medium | high`, verifying the sanitizer is invoked with the correct `thinkingLevel` and that an end-to-end run with the real sanitizer replaces `thinkingBudget: -1` with `thinkingLevel: MINIMAL`.
- `src/agents/simple-completion-transport.test.ts` gains an integration case asserting `google-generative-ai` models are routed through `prepareGoogleSimpleCompletionModel` and end up on the OpenClaw alias api.

Existing `google-stream-wrappers.test.ts` cases for `gemini-flash-latest` already prove the sanitizer's behavior.

Fixes #84688

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Control UI could restore a historical routed session key like `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` from persisted local settings after reload.
- Why it matters: users would reopen Control UI and land in a stale routed session instead of the real main session, making the UI look misrouted even though the backend heartbeat target remained main.
- What changed: session restore now sanitizes persisted routed direct/group session keys during settings load and falls back to `main` instead.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway routing behavior, heartbeat behavior, or live session switching logic was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61661
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI restoring historical routed direct/group sessions from persisted local settings instead of falling back to main.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm vitest run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts ... falls back to main when restoring a routed direct session
✓ ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts ... falls back to main when restoring a routed group session
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       18 passed (18)
```
- Observed result after fix: persisted `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` session selections are normalized back to `main` during settings restore.
- What was not tested: explicit session selection through URL query params or live backend routing changes, because this PR only changes local restore behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Control UI storage restore trusted persisted `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` values without filtering out historical routed direct/group sessions.
- Missing detection / guardrail: storage tests covered scoped session persistence but did not cover stale routed-session restore behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): main-session normalization already existed later in the gateway boot path, but the bad routed session had already won at the storage restore layer.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: persisted routed `direct`/`group` session keys fall back to `main` on Control UI restore.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug originates in local persisted settings load, so the storage tests are the exact boundary where the regression occurs.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds two restore-specific cases for routed `direct` and `group` sessions.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

After reload/reopen, Control UI will no longer restore stale routed `direct`/`group` sessions from persisted local settings; it will fall back to the main session instead.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI local storage restore

### Steps

1. Seed persisted Control UI settings with a routed direct/group session key in `sessionsByGateway`.
2. Call `loadSettings()`.
3. Verify restored `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` fall back to `main`.

### Expected

- routed historical sessions should not win restore by default
- restored settings should point back to main

### Actual

- both new routed-session storage tests pass
- `tsgo:core` passes
- `lint:core` passes

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the restore sanitization happens in `ui/src/ui/storage.ts`, which is the correct layer for this bug.
- Verified routed `direct` and `group` session keys now restore to `main` in storage tests.
- Verified core type/lint checks pass on the changed files.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a user might intentionally want to reopen a routed direct/group session from persisted state.
  - Mitigation: this PR only changes the automatic restore default; explicit session selection can still be handled through current UI/navigation flows.
- Risk: over-matching could reset legitimate session keys.
  - Mitigation: the sanitization is narrowly limited to `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` patterns only.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  ui  ../../ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts (22 tests) 38ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  22 passed (22)
    Start at  00:14:43
    Duration  611ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Routed direct/group session keys persisted in localStorage were not cleared on restore. The regex `/^agent:[^:]+:[^:]+:(direct|group):.+$/` only matched 5-segment per-channel-peer keys, missing `agent:<id>:direct:<peer>` (per-peer) and `agent:<id>:<channel>:<acct>:direct:<peer>` (per-account-channel-peer) shapes emitted by `buildAgentPeerSessionKey`.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, jsdom, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 22/22 tests pass including two new tests covering per-peer (`agent:main:direct:ou_123`) and per-account-channel-peer (`agent:main:feishu:acct_abc:direct:ou_123`) shapes, both correctly falling back to `main`.
**Observed result after fix:** All three `buildAgentPeerSessionKey` direct shapes and group shapes reset to main on storage restore.
**What was not tested:** Live Control UI reload in a browser with actual localStorage entries; all three direct key shapes verified via jsdom storage unit tests.

## Current-head follow-up proof — 2026-05-20

Behavior or issue addressed:
- Covers ClawSweeper's threaded per-peer routed session gap for agent:<id>:direct:<peer>:thread:<id> and legacy dm equivalents.

Real environment tested:
- PR branch fix/control-ui-routed-session-restore at commit 156d49e17c.
- Local Control UI storage module executed through jsdom with real loadSettings() and browser-like localStorage/sessionStorage.
- Full Chromium proof was attempted, but this container's headless Chromium cannot launch because libglib-2.0.so.0 is unavailable; the prior connected-browser proof still covers the Control UI reload path for the earlier provider-scoped routed shape.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
~~~text
COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s vitest run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
git diff --check
node --import tsx <jsdom loadSettings proof script>
~~~

Evidence after fix:
~~~text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  33 passed (33)

tsgo core/extensions test completed successfully
git diff --check completed successfully

{"currentHead":"156d49e17c","seededSession":"agent:main:direct:ou_123:thread:99","loadedGatewayUrl":"wss://gateway.example:8443","restoredSessionKey":"main","restoredLastActiveSessionKey":"main","restoredToMain":true}
{"currentHead":"156d49e17c","seededSession":"agent:main:dm:12345:thread:12345:99","loadedGatewayUrl":"wss://gateway.example:8443","restoredSessionKey":"main","restoredLastActiveSessionKey":"main","restoredToMain":true}
~~~

Observed result after fix:
- Threaded per-peer direct and legacy dm routed session keys now restore to main for both sessionKey and lastActiveSessionKey.

What was not tested:
- A fresh current-head Chromium screenshot was not captured because Playwright's bundled Chromium cannot start in this container due the missing libglib-2.0.so.0 system library.

target.title: fix(auth): add --token flag to paste-token and surface validTypes in invalid_type warning
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #63042. Improves non-interactive auth setup and debuggability of auth profile load errors.

## Changes

- Adds `openclaw models auth paste-token --token <value>` for trusted scripted setup.
- Keeps the existing interactive `paste-token` prompt when `--token` is omitted.
- Rejects empty `--token` values before persisting credentials.
- Validates Anthropic setup-token values before persisting them.
- Includes accepted `validTypes` in invalid auth-profile warning logs.
- Documents `--token` and its argv exposure tradeoff in `docs/cli/models.md`.
- Adds an Unreleased changelog entry for the user-facing CLI fix.

## Security note

`--token <value>` is a convenience path for trusted shells and automation, but inline secrets can be exposed through shell history, command echoing, CI logs, or process-list tools. The CLI help and docs now say this explicitly and recommend the interactive prompt when possible.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `models auth paste-token --token <value>` now runs through the real CLI without opening the interactive token prompt, updates the auth profile, and prints the normal config/profile update output.

Real environment tested: Local checkout of PR head `57874b9b64034f7b49719067b03f29683c909b33` on 2026-05-08, running the real OpenClaw CLI through `corepack pnpm openclaw` with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home/.openclaw \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
  corepack pnpm openclaw models auth paste-token \
    --provider openai \
    --profile-id proof:manual \
    --token proof-redacted-token
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the real CLI command above:

```console
Updated $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Auth profile: proof:manual (openai/token)
```

Observed result after fix: The command completed successfully without an interactive prompt and wrote the requested `proof:manual` OpenAI token auth profile in the isolated OpenClaw home.

What was not tested: A real provider token was not used; the changed behavior under test is CLI non-interactive token ingestion and profile persistence, not provider-side authentication.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/cli/models-cli.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/models-cli.ts src/commands/models/auth.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted.ts src/cli/models-cli.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts docs/cli/models.md CHANGELOG.md`
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] Real CLI proof above shows `models auth paste-token --token` completes without an interactive prompt.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
